arts.ttl: Monday's Transfer Talk Update: Carney Chukwuemeka, Tijjani Reijnders, Theo Hernandez
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up the latest confirmed transfer news and speculation from across the footballing world.

arts.ttl: Revealed: Man United make fresh contact over beating Chelsea to world class forward transfer
arts.sum: Manchester United have recently made a fresh approach to ask for information over the potential transfer of Victor Osimhen, who also remains on Chelsea’s radar. Sources with a close understanding of Osimhen’s situation have informed CaughtOffside that the Nigeria international is emerging as a top target for Man Utd due to the doubts over Marcus […] The post Revealed: Man United make fresh contact over beating Chelsea to world class forward transfer appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: When Chelsea expect Romeo Lavia to return from injury after missing 2-1 win vs Brentford – journalist
arts.sum: Chelsea played their first Premier League game of the season where they were actually facing a real pile up of injuries. As well as Pedro...

arts.ttl: 'Some talent': Pundit blown away by Chelsea player who reminded him of Chris Waddle against Brentford
arts.sum: Chelsea are going along nicely under Enzo Maresca and the onlooking pundits are salivating over a couple of their players at the moment. Maresca is...

arts.ttl: 'Worked really hard': Enzo Maresca delighted with 'unbelievable' Chelsea player after his display vs Brentford
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca watched his side extend their winning run during Sunday night’s victory over Brentford. Chelsea made it five consecutive wins in...

arts.ttl: Every word Enzo Maresca said on Chelsea title race, Marc Cucurella red card, Brentford win
arts.sum: "No matter how many games we are going to win, I think we are not ready to compete for the title. I've told you many times I would love the pressure to compete for titles but we are not ready for many reasons; one of the reasons why is because we cannot concede the goal we conceded, open the game and give them the chance to take a point here."

arts.ttl: How many games Marc Cucurella will miss for Chelsea after red card vs Brentford
arts.sum: Therefore, Enzo Maresca will be without the left-back for next Sunday's Premier League clash away at Everton. Marc Cucurella will miss Chelsea's clash against Everton next weekend after being sent off against Brentford on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Marc Cucurella breaks silence with 12-word message after Chelsea red card vs Brentford
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella has broken his silence after he was shown a red card after the full-time whistle following Chelsea's win over Brentford. The defender was shown a second yellow card after the Blues' 2-1 win over the Bees on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Chelsea the weekend's big winners as Europe's giants stumble
arts.sum: How else could Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester City, Barcelona, Real Madrid, Juventus and Bayern Munich all dropping points be explained? Santa Claus delivered pretty much the perfect weekend: a 2-1 win for the Blues against Brentford at Stamford Bridge, while rivals Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester City and Aston Villa all dropped points.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca reveals why he feels Chelsea can't compete for the title despite moving just two points behind Liverpool with win over Brentford
arts.sum: The Blues secured a 2-1 win at home to Brentford on Sunday to close the gap on league leaders Liverpool to just two points, although Arne Slot's side have a game in hand.
